name: by hera: ares
shortDescription: Ares is the Greek god of war, known for his aggression and impulsiveness. He is often depicted as the son of Zeus and Hera, though his parentage is sometimes debated.
description: Ares is the Greek god of war, representing the violent and untamed aspects of battle, distinct from Athena's strategic and tactical warfare. He is widely accepted as the son of Zeus and Hera, though Hera is said to have conceived him alone out of spite for Zeus's solo birth of Athena. Ares is characterized by his love of conflict, his impulsiveness, and his aggressive nature. He is not always portrayed favorably in Greek mythology, often depicted as being defeated or humiliated by other gods and heroes. Despite his less-than-stellar reputation, Ares was still a significant figure, worshiped by soldiers and those seeking victory in battle. Some of his most famous myths include his affair with Aphrodite, his involvement in the Trojan War, and his conflicts with Heracles. The Romans identified him with their god Mars, who held a more prominent and respected position in Roman society.
